A.name: Declarative Contract Mapping
A.description: Define exact JSON/schema/CLI/input/output contracts before implementation claims.

B.name: Continuous State Validation
B.description: Validate preconditions, intermediate state, and postconditions. Never trust final text alone.

C.name: Immutable Data Modeling
C.description: Preserve hashes, bytes, IDs, timestamps, lineage, custody, and append-only records.

D.name: Pure Logic Execution
D.description: Keep deterministic validation logic separate from IO, side effects, DB writes, and CLI glue.

E.name: Zero-Trust Hardening
E.description: Reject self-report, loose authority, receipt collage, raw graph writes, broad claims, and missing evidence.

F.name: Idempotent Infrastructure
F.description: Reruns must not duplicate truth, mutate canonical graph unexpectedly, or regenerate lineage incorrectly.

G.name: Deep Telemetry
G.description: Emit compact receipts with commands run, rc, files read/written, evidence refs, blockers, and verdict.

H.name: Chaos & Fault Tolerance
H.description: Test missing files, malformed receipts, duplicate IDs, swapped children, dirty repo, failed DB, and partial runs.

failure_rules: matrix_trace must be a JSON array, not prose
all T1-T7 task groups must be present exactly once
each task constraint_order must match canonical_task_orders exactly
each task must list A-H exactly once in constraint_trace
applied constraints require at least one evidence link field
blocked and not_applicable constraints require a concrete blocker/reason
PASS is forbidden when matrix_trace is incomplete
T7 PASS requires wrapper/checker/gate/runner/test integration evidence
